Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following option(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
• Split spoon sampler has a larger area ratio, so with the sampler having a thicker wall, it is impossible to get the undisturbed sample.
• Due to soil disturbances during pile installation and pile stress zone overlap, the settlement
of a group of friction piles is greater than the settlement of a single pile.

Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A hammer is used to drive a wooden pile. The hammer weight is 30 kN and its freefall height is 0.75 m. The penetration in the last blow is 12 mm. The correct options regarding load carrying capacity of pile are:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, d)

 
 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In a 16 piles group in a square pattern; pile diameter is 45 cm and centre to centre spacing of the square group is 1.5 m. Given C = 50 kN/m2, length of each pile is 10 m, negligible bearing at the tip of the pile, adhesion coefficient between pile and soil is 0.7. Which of the following option(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, d)

 
 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A group of 16 piles, in a square pattern of 4 × 4 and each individual pile are having the estimated ultimate load capacity of single pile and group is 170 kN and 2700 kN respectively. The efficiency of pile group will be

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A pile of 50 cm diameter and 5 m length, is driven in a uniform clay of unconfined compressive strength 90 kN/m2. The safe frictional resistance of the pile is:
(Take: Adhesion factor (α) = 0.6 and FOS = 3)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A soil sample is collected using a sampling tube that is driven 450 mm into the ground, the length of the soil sample recovered in the tube is found to be 405 mm. What does this indicate about the behaviour of the soil during sampling?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A circular footing is resting on surface of saturated clay of unconfined compressive strength of 200 kN/m2. If safe load carrying capacity of circular footing is 450 kN, then required diameter of circular footing a (as per Terzaghi’s theory) is ________ m. (Correct upto two decimal places)
(Take: Factor of safety = 2.5)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.39
Solution

1.39 (1.35 - 1.45)

 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In plate load test, the plate of size 500 mm square settles by 20 mm in sandy soil. If permissible settlement of footing in sandy soil is 30 mm, then width of footing is ____ mm. (Round off to next higher integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 980
Solution

980 (978 - 982)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A differential free swell test on a soil gives the value of differential free swell of 25%. What is the degree of swelling?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A 12 m long and 400 mm diameter concrete pile is driven in uniform deposit of dense sand with dry unit weight of sand as 19.6 kN/m3. (Neglect arching effect)
The safe load carrying capacity of this single pile will be

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The observed ‘No’ value in the field is 50 at a depth of 5 m. The average saturated unit weight of fine sand is 20 kN/m3. The corrected N value of the soil is ______. (in integer).

NAT
Correct answer: 44
Solution

44 (43 - 45)

 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A rectangular footing measuring 2 m × 3 m is placed on a soil stratum that is 6m thick. The soil has a Poisson’s ratio (μ) = 0.25 and Young’s modulus(E) = 20 MN/m2 and influence factor (If) = 1.1. If the immediate settlement of the footing is observed to be 10.31 mm, then determine the load carried by the footing at ground level _____kN. (Round off to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 600
Solution

600 (598 - 602)

 
 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

For a group of 16 piles in square pattern as shown in the figure, the settlement of pile group, in a normally consolidated clay structum having properties as shown below will be _____ mm. (Correct upto two decmial places). Take load dispersion 2H : 1V

 
NAT
Correct answer: 35.91
Solution

35.91 (32 - 37)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A group of 9 piles of 8 m length and 500 mm in diameter each is embedded in a clay soil with an unconfined compressive strength of 70 kN/m2. Group efficiency factor is 1 and adhesion factor is 0.8. If neglecting bearing at the tip of the piles, centre to centre distance of the piles will be _______ m. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.16
Solution

1.16 (1.10 - 1.50)

A shallow circular foundation of diameter 2.5 m is founded 1.2 m below the gound surface.

 
 
For local shear failure, the shear strength parameters are C = 20 kN/m2, = 15° and bearing capacity factors are NC = 12, Nq = 8, Nγ = 6. The ultimate bearing capacity for this footing is
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
 
Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A raft of 5 m × 8 m is laid at a depth of 2 m in a cohesive soil having undrained shear strength of 80 kN/m2. The net ultimate bearing capacity of soil using Terzaghi’s theory is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
